<!-- GENERATED by `bun run gen` from scenarios/mysql/02-isolation/non-repeatable-read.ts — do not edit by hand. -->

```
A> SET TRANSACTION ISOLATION LEVEL READ COMMITTED;
Query OK

A> BEGIN;
Query OK

A> SELECT balance FROM accounts WHERE id = 1;
 balance 
---------
     100 
(1 row)
```

*While A's transaction is still open, B updates the row and commits.*

```
B> UPDATE accounts SET balance = 200 WHERE id = 1;
Query OK, 1 row affected

A> SELECT balance FROM accounts WHERE id = 1;
 balance 
---------
     200 
(1 row)

A> COMMIT;
Query OK
```

*Readers never block — but writers do. The same interleaving with UPDATEs makes B wait for A's row lock.*

```
A> BEGIN;
Query OK

A> UPDATE accounts SET balance = 300 WHERE id = 1;
Query OK, 1 row affected

B> UPDATE accounts SET balance = 400 WHERE id = 1;
⏳ B is waiting for a lock…

A> COMMIT;
Query OK

⏵ B resumes:
Query OK, 1 row affected

B> SELECT balance FROM accounts WHERE id = 1;
 balance 
---------
     400 
(1 row)
```

<small>Verified against MySQL 8.4.10 · [Run it yourself](/about/run-locally) · [Scenario source](https://github.com/svyatov/database-transactions/blob/main/scenarios/mysql/02-isolation/non-repeatable-read.ts)</small>
